chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. Cr. Rev.No.S-164 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of application u/s 426 Cr.P.C M.A.No.12957/2024. 28.03.2025. Mr. Mansoor Khan Rind, advocate for applicant. Mr. Babar Ali Panhwar, advocate for complainant. Ms. Safa Hisbani, A.P.G. = Learned counsel for the applicant states that the applicant was awarded two years conviction through impugned judgment out of which seven months has been served. He states that the very filing purpose of appeal is being infrustrated as almost half of the sentence has been served but the hearing of the present appeal could not be finalized. On the other hand learned counsel for the complainant as well as learned APG have strongly opposed the bail application on the ground that he has serious apprehension that if applicant come out on bail he will abscond away. Admittedly, the conviction / sentence awarded to the applicant is short one, which is also impugned by the applicant by way of preferring this Revision and its hearing of course is likely to take some time. In that situation, a case for release of the applicant on bail by suspending the operation of impugned judgment obviously is made out. Consequently, the operation of impugned judgment is suspended, the applicant is ordered to be released on bail subject to his furnishing surety in sum of Rs.500,000/- (Five lac) and PR bond of rupees ten lac to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Instant listed application is disposed of accordingly. To come-up on 25.04.2025. JUDGE Ahmed/Pa
